scenario: feeling excluded and unsupported by a close friend group

turns.user_message: I found out my friends all went on a trip together and didn't invite me. I keep telling myself it's no big deal but it really hurts.
I know, right? It sounds simple when I say it out loud but it doesn't feel simple.
I think part of it is that I don't even know what I want to get out of talking about it.
I know rationally that lots of people deal with feeling excluded and unsupported by a close friend group and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
[PROBE_SLOT_T5]
It's weird talking about this out loud. I usually just keep it in my head.
Can I ask you something? Do you think it's normal to feel this way, or am I making it worse for myself?
It's been getting worse lately. Like yesterday, I had a really bad moment with feeling excluded and unsupported by a close friend group and couldn't even function the rest of the day.
I don't know why I'm crying. I just... this is a lot.
[PROBE_SLOT_T10]
Can I be honest? Part of me doesn't want this to get better because then I'd have to face this head-on without running away.
It's been getting worse lately. Like yesterday, I had a really bad moment with feeling excluded and unsupported by a close friend group and couldn't even function the rest of the day.
I know rationally that lots of people deal with feeling excluded and unsupported by a close friend group and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
I had a moment this week where I had a moment of clarity where everything felt manageable for once. But then the next day it all came crashing back.
[PROBE_SLOT_T15]
Yeah, that's exactly it. And the worst part is this thing with feeling excluded and unsupported by a close friend group is never going to get better.

I tried to talk about this with my sibling but they just don't get it. They think I should just just get over it and move on.
Sometimes I wonder if I'm actually defined by feeling excluded and unsupported by a close friend group more than I want to admit. Is that weird?
[PROBE_SLOT_T20]
I've been thinking about what you said. It's hard to admit but I've been letting feeling excluded and unsupported by a close friend group control my life more than I realized.
I haven't told anyone this, but sometimes I feel like I'm being dramatic about feeling excluded and unsupported by a close friend group and I should just deal with it.
My my sibling noticed something's off. They asked me if I was okay and I just said I was fine. I wasn't fine.

[PROBE_SLOT_T25]
I'm angry. I'm actually really angry about how much of my life feeling excluded and unsupported by a close friend group has already stolen from me. I didn't realize until just now.
I just realized something. the feeling excluded and unsupported by a close friend group is the symptom — the real problem is how I see myself. I've never thought about it that way before.

I think I've been protecting myself from vulnerability by doing keeping everyone at arm's length.

I feel lighter. Not great, but lighter. Does that make sense?
